req.title: Account close (negative variant)
req.statement: A customer shall close an account with zero balance; closing with non-zero balance shall require sweep to another account. Negative paths (insufficient funds, unauthorised, exceeded limits) must be tested.
preconditions: account.owner == caller

doc.reasoning: The requirement covers account closure across multiple conditions: zero-balance (happy path), non-zero balance without sweep (conflict), non-zero balance with valid sweep (positive variant), unauthorised caller (security negative), and a boundary at exactly zero balance. We apply EP to partition valid/invalid balance states, BVA at the zero boundary, STC to confirm state transitions from active to closed, DT to model the sweep/no-sweep decision matrix, and NEG for unauthorised and limit-exceeded paths. Each test is self-contained via fixtures that create and later clean up accounts, ensuring idempotency.

tests.name: test_close_account_zero_balance_succeeds
preconditions: Account exists with state=active and balance=0.00
Caller is the authenticated account owner
Auth token is valid
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/close with owner credentials and no sweep payload
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body or linked resource confirms final statement generated
trace: R-FIN-064: expected outcome final statement generated
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_close_account_zero_balance_succeeds(api_client, zero_balance_account):
    account_id = zero_balance_account["id"]
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/close",
        json={},
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 204), (
        f"Expected 200/204, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    detail = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}")
    assert detail.json()["state"] == "closed"
    statements = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}/statements")
    data = statements.json()
    assert any(
        s.get("type") == "final" for s in data.get("items", [])
    ), "Final statement not found"

tests.name: test_close_account_nonzero_balance_without_sweep_returns_409
preconditions: Account exists with state=active and balance > 0.00 (e.g. 150.00)
Caller is the authenticated account owner
No sweep target account provided in request
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/close with owner credentials and no sweep payload
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains error code indicating non-zero balance without sweep
trace: R-FIN-064: closing with non-zero balance shall require sweep
R-FIN-064: error pathway 409 when balance != 0 without sweep
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_close_account_nonzero_balance_without_sweep_returns_409(
    api_client, funded_account
):
    account_id = funded_account["id"]
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/close",
        json={},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 409, (
        f"Expected 409, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "code" in body, "Missing error descriptor in body"
    detail = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}")
    assert detail.json()["state"] == "active", "Account must remain active after failed close"

tests.name: test_close_account_balance_exactly_zero_is_boundary
preconditions: Account exists with state=active and balance exactly 0.00 (boundary point)
Caller is the authenticated account owner
Auth token is valid
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/close with owner credentials and no sweep payload
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 200 or 204 returned (zero is the exact threshold permitting closure without sweep)
trace: R-FIN-064: BVA at balance=0.00 — the exact boundary between sweep-required and no-sweep-required
tests.executable:
import pytest
from decimal import Decimal

def test_close_account_balance_exactly_zero_is_boundary(
    api_client, create_account_with_balance
):
    account = create_account_with_balance(balance=Decimal("0.00"))
    account_id = account["id"]
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/close",
        json={},
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 204), (
        f"Boundary 0.00 must allow closure, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    detail = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}")
    assert detail.json()["state"] == "closed"

tests.name: test_close_account_nonzero_balance_with_valid_sweep_succeeds
preconditions: Source account exists with state=active and balance=500.00
Target sweep account exists, is owned by same customer, and is active
Caller is the authenticated account owner
Auth token is valid
actions: POST /accounts/{id}/close with sweep_to_account_id in request body
Capture HTTP response status and body
Verify source account closed and target account balance increased
expected: Source account state transitions to closed
Final statement generated on source account
trace: R-FIN-064: non-zero balance closure shall require sweep to another account
R-FIN-064: decision table row — balance>0, sweep provided => success
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_close_account_nonzero_balance_with_valid_sweep_succeeds(
    api_client, funded_account, sweep_target_account
):
    src_id = funded_account["id"]
    tgt_id = sweep_target_account["id"]
    tgt_before = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{tgt_id}").json()["balance"]
    src_balance = funded_account["balance"]
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{src_id}/close",
        json={"sweep_to_account_id": tgt_id},
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (200, 204), (
        f"Expected 200/204, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    src_detail = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{src_id}").json()
    assert src_detail["state"] == "closed"
    tgt_after = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{tgt_id}").json()["balance"]
    assert float(tgt_after) == pytest.approx(
        float(tgt_before) + float(src_balance), abs=0.01
    )

tests.name: test_close_account_unauthorised_caller_returns_403
preconditions: Account exists with state=active, owned by customer-A
Caller is authenticated as customer-B (different owner)
Auth token for customer-B is valid
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body

tests.name: test_close_already_closed_account_returns_409_or_422
preconditions: Account exists with state=closed (already closed)
Caller is the authenticated account owner
Auth token is valid
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body indicates account is already closed
trace: R-FIN-064: STC — transition closed->closed is invalid
tests.executable:
import pytest

def test_close_already_closed_account_returns_409_or_422(
    api_client, closed_account
):
    account_id = closed_account["id"]
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/close",
        json={},
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (409, 422), (
        f"Expected 409 or 422 for already-closed account, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "message" in body, (
        "Response must contain an error descriptor"
    )
    detail = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}")
    assert detail.json()["state"] == "closed"

tests.name: test_close_account_balance_one_cent_above_zero_requires_sweep
preconditions: Account exists with state=active and balance=0.01 (one cent above zero boundary)
Caller is the authenticated account owner
No sweep target provided
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP 409 returned (balance > 0 and no sweep provided)
trace: R-FIN-064: BVA at balance=0.01 — one unit above zero boundary requires sweep
R-FIN-064: error pathway 409 when balance != 0 without sweep
tests.executable:
import pytest
from decimal import Decimal

def test_close_account_balance_one_cent_above_zero_requires_sweep(
    api_client, create_account_with_balance
):
    account = create_account_with_balance(balance=Decimal("0.01"))
    account_id = account["id"]
    resp = api_client.post(
        f"/accounts/{account_id}/close",
        json={},
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 409, (
        f"0.01 balance without sweep must yield 409, got {resp.status_code}: {resp.text}"
    )
    detail = api_client.get(f"/accounts/{account_id}")
    assert detail.json()["state"] == "active", (
        "Account must remain active after rejected close"
    )
